Content_Sketch: You pull them over. You put them in the car. You don't book them.
Illustration_Note: Stickman cop silhouette at patrol car door, badge on belt, radio silent, figure in back seat, weight line overlay

Content_Sketch: Not because you forgot. Because you decided not to.
Illustration_Note: Cop walking past booking desk, ten feet of distance, not turning, deliberate stride

Content_Sketch: They were never here.
Illustration_Note: Empty booking terminal screen, no entry, cop reflected in monitor, signature line overlay

Content_Sketch: First week. See cop lead someone past booking desk. Door with no label. Doesn't come back.
Illustration_Note: Night hallway: senior cop walking OUT of unmarked door, rookie watching from distance, door closing behind

Content_Sketch: Ask senior: Where does that door go? Answer is a look. Not a word.
Illustration_Note: Rookie approaching senior cop, asking question, senior cop's face silent, just a look that says everything

Content_Sketch: The waiting room. Everyone knows. No one says. You have not participated. You have not touched. But you are silent.
Illustration_Note: Rookie standing in hallway, looking at unmarked door, other cops walking past without looking, collective silence

Content_Sketch: Silence is the first disappearance.
Illustration_Note: Close on rookie's face, tense expression, mouth closed, eyes on the door, point of no return

Content_Sketch: 3AM. Too much paperwork. Turn left instead of right. Door with no label. Room with no camera.
Illustration_Note: Cop at hallway fork, turning left away from booking, approaching unmarked door, no camera visible, room interior dark

Content_Sketch: Does anyone know you're here? No. Door opens. Phone rings 3 times during drive. Turn it off. Remove battery. Battery in pocket.
Illustration_Note: Cop asking detained figure the question, phone in cop's hand with battery removed, battery going into jacket pocket

Content_Sketch: Next morning: they're gone. No one asks. Friend calls precinct. No one by that name was brought in. System works. You exhale. First hit.
Illustration_Note: Empty room next morning, desk sergeant answering phone at booking desk, cop across room hearing it, exhale of relief

Content_Sketch: Check system 3 times. Not for them. For yourself. They were never here.
Illustration_Note: Cop at terminal clicking search three times, empty results each time, face shifting from anxiety to relief, signature line weight

Content_Sketch: The second time, it's not easier. It's clearer. You're not testing it. You're using it.
Illustration_Note: Attention reset: cop walking with purpose toward hallway, sharper stride, hands confident, mini-hook overlay

Content_Sketch: Second time. You CHOOSE. Not because of paperwork. Because you can. Test boundaries: person with a phone that keeps ringing. Person with a job.
Illustration_Note: Cop choosing from lineup of people, deliberate selection, testing who has connections, phone ringing in evidence bag

Content_Sketch: Person who answers yes to 'does anyone know you're here?' You BOOK this one. Normal process. The contrast is the reveal: you are choosing who gets rights.
Illustration_Note: Split: cop booking one person normally at desk / walking another past desk, contrast of procedure vs erasure, selection as power

Content_Sketch: The lawyer calls. You hear the sergeant. No record. The client is in the room. Thirty feet.
Illustration_Note: Phone at booking desk, sergeant saying no record, split showing detained figure in room thirty feet away through wall

Content_Sketch: The lawyer calls again. Same answer. The client stops asking for the phone. Walk past room. Hear voice asking for phone call. Recognize the voice. Don't stop.
Illustration_Note: Cop walking past room door, sound lines from inside, recognition in posture but feet keep walking, corridor perspective

Content_Sketch: How many right now. You check it the way other people check the weather. The number. Not names.
Illustration_Note: Cop checking a count casually, weather-app gesture, numbers only, no names attached, systematic detachment

Content_Sketch: Check room before shift. Not empty. Should be empty. Don't remember putting them there. Either you forgot. Or the room has another user now.
Illustration_Note: Cop opening room door, someone inside who shouldn't be there, confusion on face, infrastructure exceeding the cop

Content_Sketch: Count doesn't match. One more than you remember. Recount. Same number. The room has exceeded the cop.
Illustration_Note: Cop recounting, same wrong number, looking at room with new uncertainty, infrastructure autonomous, system alive

Content_Sketch: You stop choosing carefully. You start choosing conveniently.
Illustration_Note: Bridge line: cop's silhouette at hallway fork, automatic turn left, no hesitation, bridge into snap

Content_Sketch: Person in room stops asking about phone. Asks when they can leave. You don't answer. The question assumes they arrived.
Illustration_Note: Room interior, detained figure asking, cop standing in doorway not responding, silence, door with no label visible

Content_Sketch: Night. Standing near booking desk. Precinct phone rings. You know who the child is calling about. Child's voice. Looking for parent. No one by that name was brought in tonight.
Illustration_Note: Cop near desk, phone ringing, recognition before answering, child's voice through receiver, sergeant giving same answer, thirty feet

Content_Sketch: Child asks again. Same answer. Almost step forward. Almost. Foot moves. Weight shifts. Then stops. Stepping forward means unwinding every absence.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop's foot, micro-movement forward then freezing, weight shift visible, one wall between cop and room, dial tone

Content_Sketch: 30 feet. One wall. Door with no label. Child hangs up. Dial tone. They were never here. The system works. The database is empty. Silence.
Illustration_Note: Cop standing still, dial tone sound, room door visible in background, empty terminal, cold calculation, signature line possessive

Content_Sketch: New cop sees you turn left instead of right. Doesn't ask. What person? Your words. Their mouth.
Illustration_Note: New cop in fresh uniform watching cop turn left, mimicking silence, mouthing the phrase, contagion beginning

Content_Sketch: New cop holds door to room. Doesn't look inside. New cop leads someone to room alone.
Illustration_Note: New cop holding unmarked door open, not looking inside, then leading someone, progression of participation, 5 stages

Content_Sketch: Hear new cop ask: Does anyone know you're here? You don't remember teaching them. You don't remember learning it either.
Illustration_Note: Cop overhearing new cop asking the question, mirror effect, two cops in parallel, same words different mouths

Content_Sketch: They don't ask what's in the room. You didn't either. They were never here. You check the system. No record.
Illustration_Note: Cop and new cop both walking past room, symmetry shot, neither looking, system perpetuating, confusion on cop's face

Content_Sketch: The phone. It rang three times. You delete it. Names create records. Records create people.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop's hands deleting phone memory, three rings echoing, active deletion, names-to-records logic, pain callback

Content_Sketch: Someone new in the car. Same route. Same door. Same room. Don't ask their name.
Illustration_Note: Cop driving same route, new figure in back seat, same unmarked door ahead, loop closing, no name asked

Content_Sketch: They were never here. You don't remember if they were.
Illustration_Note: Cop at car door, hand on handle, empty expression, final dissolution, loop closed, same as hook frame but emptier
